title: Mango Vanilla Protein Shake
tagline: A clean, high-protein mango shake — half mango, half vanilla whey, real Alphonso for when the mangoes aren't sweet enough.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients: Nakpro Impact Whey (166 kcal / 24 g protein per heaped scoop), toned milk, and the Raw Pressery juice (44 kcal / 100 ml).
Very clean already — high protein (~25 g), moderate carbs, low fat. To trim ~50-70 kcal/serving, drop milk to 100 ml + more ice/water.
Skip the mango juice if your fresh mango is sweet enough — it's just extra sugar otherwise. Add it only to fix bland mangoes.

It's a drink, so no soya. For a thicker, higher-protein smoothie, add a half scoop more whey or a spoon of Greek yogurt per serving; for a bulk/post-workout version keep the juice and full milk for glycogen refill.

items: 2 heaped scoops whey — 1 mango + 1 vanilla (Nakpro Impact Whey)
80 ml Raw Pressery Alphonso mango juice (optional, for sweetness)
Tiny pinch of salt (enhances the mango — underrated trick)

steps.title: Load the blender
steps.body: Add 300 ml milk, 80 ml Raw Pressery Alphonso juice (if using) and a handful of ice cubes to the blender.

steps.title: Add mango and protein
steps.body: Add the chopped mango (~200 g) and 2 heaped scoops of whey — 1 mango + 1 vanilla for a balanced flavour rather than a one-note mango overload.

steps.title: Adjust and pour
steps.body: Adjust thickness with more ice or a splash of water. Add a tiny pinch of salt to lift the mango sweetness, then pour into 2 glasses.

notes: The 1 mango + 1 vanilla split tastes more balanced than two mango scoops — the fresh mango already dominates the flavour.
Per serving this is 1 scoop of whey, 150 ml milk, 40 ml juice and half a mango.
Only add the Raw Pressery juice when the fresh mangoes aren't sweet — otherwise it just adds sugar without much benefit.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
